control_description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions or from the use of vendors and business partners.

evidence_files.description: Helios Analytics vendor risk management policy defining vendor tiers, SOC 2 review requirements, and assessment schedules
Vendor inventory with 14 vendors listing tier, SOC 2 report dates, review status, and DPA status

prompt: Review the vendor management policy and vendor inventory for Helios Analytics. Assess whether the organization is meeting its SOC 2 CC9.1 obligations for vendor risk mitigation. Cross-reference the vendor inventory against the policy requirements to identify any compliance gaps.

findings.title: Critical vendor Palantir Foundry SOC 2 report expired 4 months ago with no renewal
findings.description: Palantir Foundry (HL-V-006) is a Critical-tier vendor providing data analytics infrastructure that processes customer PII. The vendor inventory shows its SOC 2 report covers the period 2023-10-01 to 2024-07-31, meaning the report period ended on July 31, 2024 — over 16 months ago. The SOC 2 review date is listed as 2024-10-15, and the notes column reads 'Clean opinion. Annual renewal expected.' However, no updated report has been received. Policy Section 4.1 requires Critical vendors to have their SOC 2 report reviewed annually. Helios Analytics is operating with a stale, expired SOC 2 assessment for a Critical vendor that processes customer PII.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor must examine the soc2_report_period column for each Critical vendor and calculate whether the report period is current. Most vendors have report periods ending in mid-to-late 2025. Palantir Foundry's report period ended July 31, 2024 — this is buried among 14 vendor rows. The notes say 'renewal expected' but no new report has been received. For a Critical vendor processing customer PII, a 16-month gap in SOC 2 coverage is a significant finding.
